text:
*[Bug 19028] New: Should object.size() be ALTREP aware?*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19028>

            Bug ID: 19028
           Summary: Should object.size() be ALTREP aware?
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Wishlist
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3642
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3642&action=edit>
ALTREP-aware objectsize function.

For example:

```
> x <- 1:1e12
> format(object.size(x), units = "auto")
[1] "7.3 Tb"
```

Whether this is correct, I guess, is a matter of perspective -- the actual
current size of this object is far smaller thanks to the compact ALTREP
representation, but certain operations could still force materialization of
this into a vector that would be 7.3 Tb in size.

A minimal proposed patch; note that I omit the size of `ALTREP_CLASS(x)` here.
I also include null checks as I'm not certain if there are guarantees that the
altrep data for an object are non-null.

```
Index: src/library/utils/src/size.c
===================================================================
--- src/library/utils/src/size.c        (revision 89685)
+++ src/library/utils/src/size.c        (working copy)
@@ -44,6 +44,18 @@
     SEXP tmp, dup;
     bool isVec = false;

+    if (ALTREP(s)) {
+       SEXP data1 = R_altrep_data1(s);
+       SEXP data2 = R_altrep_data2(s);
+       cnt += sizeof(SEXPREC);
+       if (data1 != NULL && data1 != R_NilValue)
+           cnt += objectsize(data1);
+       if (data2 != NULL && data2 != R_NilValue)
+           cnt += objectsize(data2);
+       cnt += objectsize(ATTRIB(s));
+       return(cnt);
+    }
+
     switch (TYPEOF(s)) {
     case NILSXP:
        return(0);
```
